message:
fix(redis,raptorq): prevent integer overflow in RESP parser and symbol params

Redis RESP bulk-string parser: replace unchecked `end_data + 2` with
`end_data.saturating_add(2)` to prevent arithmetic overflow when
parsing extremely large (or malicious) bulk-string length values.
The prior code could panic on debug builds or silently wrap on release
when `end_data` was near `usize::MAX`.

Redis pipeline: call `conn.ensure_initialized(cx)` before writing
pipelined commands so AUTH/SELECT are always issued on freshly
acquired connections. Without this, pipelines on unauthenticated or
wrong-database connections would fail silently or return errors from
the server that were hard to correlate with the missing init step.

RaptorQ typed_symbol: replace bare `as u8` / `as u16` casts in
`object_params_for_payload` with explicit `.min(T::MAX as usize)`
saturation before narrowing. Previously, extreme configurations
(very large payloads with small symbol sizes) could silently truncate
`blocks` and `symbols_per_block`, producing incorrect encoding
parameters that would corrupt data at rest.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
